articles.title: Harry Kane blazes penalty over, but Bayern heap pressure on Wolfsburg 👀
articles.description: Harry Kane MISSED a penalty, but Bayern Munich beat Wolfsburg 1-0 away from home this Saturday (9). Michael Olise scored a STUNNING decisive goal that took the champions to 86 points — runners-up Borussia Dortmund have "only" 70. Wolfsburg, meanwhile, are in an extremely delicate situation. The club sits 16th, with 26 points. Wolfsburg are outside the relegation zone only on tiebreakers — St. Pauli also have 26 points, but a worse goal difference. And the final round will feature St. Pauli vs. Wolfsburg. Whoever loses will be RELEGATED. Whoever wins will have a chance to stay up in the playoffs — Wolfsburg have the advantage of a draw. All matches in the final round will be played next Saturday (16) at 10:30 a.m. Bayern Munich will host Cologne. This article was translated into English by Artificial Intelligence. You can read the original version in 🇧🇷 here.

articles.title: 👀 Horror tackle after two minutes: Werder star sees straight red
articles.description: Not even five minutes had been played when VAR official Sören Storks called the referee over to the monitor for the first time. As the video footage showed, with good reason. Werder’s Sugawara needlessly planted his studs into the calf of Hoffenheim’s Bazoumana Touré with a straight leg. The referee’s decision was therefore the logical one: an early red card for the Japanese player. Although Werder are all but mathematically safe, going down to ten men so early still hurts. On the one hand, there is still the hypothetical possibility of being dragged back into trouble; on the other, a final away win to end the season would certainly have been a fitting way to make peace with the fans, who have had few highlights to celebrate this campaign. This article was translated into English by Artificial Intelligence. You can read the original version in 🇩🇪 here.
